title: fcp-keyg: The Final Cut Pro 1–7 binary project format — specification and extraction tools
description:
The first public specification of Apple Final Cut Pro's legacy binary project format (.fcp, magic "KeyG", 1999–2011), produced by independent clean-room reverse engineering of project data files for interoperability and digital-preservation purposes, together with open-source extraction tools (timeline recovery, media reference extraction, export to FCP7 XML importable in modern editing software).

The specification is provided in English and French. It covers file identification (magic, header, constant format signature), both byte-order variants (PowerPC big-endian and Intel little-endian), the serialized property tree data model, the dynamic per-file property identifier system, persistent object reference resolution through the project item table, sequence/track/clip structures, and Mac OS Alias media references.

Code is licensed under MIT; documentation under CC BY-SA 4.0. This is independent documentation, not affiliated with Apple Inc.
